message:
fix(runtime): improve blocking pool thread spawning heuristic, fix work-steal ordering, and distinguish join cancellation from task failure

blocking_pool: Compute idle thread count (active - busy) and spawn new
threads when pending work exceeds idle threads, rather than requiring all
threads to be busy. Correctly handles bursts even before existing threads
wake up to dequeue work.

scheduler/three_lane: Push stolen tasks in reverse order to preserve
original priority/FIFO ordering in the local fast queue. Create
TaskExecutionGuard before polling the task future so its drop cleanup
runs even if the poll panics.

task_handle: JoinFuture now distinguishes RecvError::Closed (task
sender dropped due to cancel/panic) from RecvError::Cancelled (the join
future itself was cancelled via Cx cancellation). The latter propagates
the cancellation reason with abort_with_reason for accurate diagnostics.

spawn_blocking: Fix test to poll both handles concurrently via
future::zip since spawn_blocking is lazy and sequential await would
serialize the tasks.

state: Update oneshot receivers to mut for new &mut self recv API;
replace Vec::with_capacity(regions_len) with Vec::new() in
cancel_tree_from since most cancel trees are small.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
